title: TypeScript Tests, AWS Faults, Release, and Production Defense · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: An engineering, security, accessibility, privacy, finance, and operations review board. The board needs @aws-sdk/lib-storage capacity evidence, strict and runtime validation, replication and failover, duplicate-safe events, bounded telemetry, budget controls, handle and stream cleanup, controlled AWS fault and restore drills, immutable canaries, rollback, and residual-risk ownership. Planned artifact: Deliver Global SaaS Asset Platform Production Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense…
objectives: Bound aggregate Upload queueSize workers, part buffers, active source streams, sockets, progress listeners, AbortControllers, descriptors, and caller admission across simultaneous transfers.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, configure versioning, destination ownership, IAM, KMS, filters, delete-marker behavior, replication status, metrics, and failure remediation.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, consume S3 notifications through supported destinations with duplicate, ordering, filtering, recursion, schema, delay, and lost-consumer recovery limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Transfer Resource Budget
steps.instruction:
project case — A project TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 1734 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate release on strict, runtime, property, leak, AWS, fault, load, restore, and rollback evidence; keep AbortSignal and stream cleanup under one named owner; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound aggregate Upload queueSize workers, part buffers, active source streams, sockets, progress listeners, AbortControllers, descriptors, and caller admission across simultaneous transfers.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Transfer Resource Budget.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bound aggregate upload queuesize workers, part buffers, active source streams, sockets, progress listeners, abortcontrollers, descriptors, and caller admission across simultaneous transfers. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Transfer Resource Budget, keep Transfer Resource Budget connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Compatible Release Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-compatible-release-defense-10v6cl" that uses case 7399 to gate release on strict, runtime, property, leak, AWS, fault, load, restore, and rollback evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
